text: Ex-Pence aide Olivia Troye plans to run for Congress in Virginia as a Democrat - The Washington Post
fullText: She joins other former Republicans and Trump administration officials running for office as critics of the president. The district would open up if voters approve a new map.

text: US wholesale prices surged 4% last month after the war in Iran sent energy prices soaring - AP News
fullText: U.S. wholesale prices surged last month as the Iran war drove up the cost of energy. The Labor Department reported Tuesday that its producer price index — which measures inflation before it hits consumers — rose 0.5% from February and 4% from March 2025. Ener…

text: Ultraprocessed foods are turning human thighs into well-marbled steaks - CNN
fullText: Diets high in ultraprocessed foods create streaks of fat in human muscles that may lead to osteoporosis, a new study found.

text: Jennie Garth Recalls Daughter Finding Her Cutting Herself Amid Dave Abrams Split - TMZ
fullText: Jennie Garth is opening up about a painful point in her life during her split from husband Dave Abrams ... she revealed she cut herself with glass while overcome with pain, and her daughter caught her in the act.

text: Live Updates: In Iran war, push to resume U.S.-Iran talks intensifies as U.S. blockade of Iran's ports enters Day 2 - CBS News
fullText: Efforts are underway to get the U.S. and Iran back to the negotiating table even as the U.S. blockade of Iran's ports enters its second day. Also, Lebanese and Israeli officials are set to meet at the State Department.

text: Stock Market Today: Oil Slips Below $100 Amid Hopes of More Talks — Live Markets - WSJ
fullText: Live updates on stocks, bonds and markets, including the Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite.

fullText: Here are five key things investors need to know to start the trading day.

text: Fresh Iran talks could begin this week as US continues blockade on ports - Fox News
fullText: President Donald Trump is committing to his naval blockade against Iran on Tuesday as the U.S. and Iran plan to resume peace talks this week. Negotiations are set to revolve around Iran's nuclear program and enrichment plans.

text: Physicists witness pinpricks of darkness moving faster than the speed of light — without breaking the laws of relativity - Live Science
fullText: For the first time, researchers measured singularities in combined light and sound waves moving faster than the speed of light. The findings have implications in fluid dynamics, optics and many other fields.

text: 'RHOA's Drew Sidora's Monthly Income Revealed in Divorce Battle - TMZ
fullText: "The Real Housewives of Atlanta" star Drew Sidora's 5-figure monthly income was exposed as part of her ugly divorce battle ... this, according to docs from her messy divorce.

text: Why Congress is fighting over a central tool of American surveillance - NPR
fullText: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act is responsible for a huge share of intel collected by the U.S. Lawmakers and civil liberties advocates are worried it enables warrantless spying on U.S. citizens.

text: GOP leaders prepare to steamroll opponents of DHS funding plan - Politico
fullText: Senate Republicans could move on a fiscal blueprint for ICE and Border Patrol funding as soon as next week.

text: Mike Trout vs. Aaron Judge turns into starry slugfest of 3-time MVPs as Yankees edge Angels 11-10 - AP News
fullText: Mike Trout and Aaron Judge turned their baseball game into a heavyweight slugfest Monday night, each hitting two home runs at Yankee Stadium. Trout's second homer gave the Angels a two-run lead in the eighth inning, but the Yankees came back to win 11-10. Tre…

text: North Korea's Kim Jong Un supervises missile tests from his naval destroyer - NBC News
fullText: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un supervised more missile tests from his prized new warship over the weekend, state media said Tuesday.
